title: Himachal: Heatwave in four stations, MeT predicts wet spell - newsdrum.in
search_term: heatwave (Bilaspur OR Chamba OR Hamirpur OR Kangra OR Kinnaur OR Kullu OR "Lahaul and Spiti" OR Mandi OR Shimla OR "Sirmaur (Sirmour)" OR Solan OR Una) Himachal Pradesh
full_text:
Shimla, Apr 25 (PTI) Temperatures in Himachal Pradesh were above normal, and a heatwave was recorded at four stations on Saturday.
Una was the hottest location, reaching a high of 42.2 degrees Celsius. This was followed by Sundernagar at 36.3 degrees Celsius, Bhuntar at 34.5 degrees Celsius, and Dharamshala at 33.1 degrees Celsius, according to the Shimla Meteorological Office.
The MeT Office has issued a yellow alert for heatwave conditions in isolated areas of Una, Kangra, Kullu, and Mandi for Sunday. Additionally, thunderstorms, lightning, and gusty winds are expected in isolated areas of five to six districts, including Chamba, Kangra, Kullu, Mandi, Shimla, and Sirmaur on Tuesday and Wednesday.
Furthermore, a wet spell is predicted for the state from April 27 to April 30. The weather remained mostly dry in the state over the past 24 hours, since Friday evening. PTI BPL MPL MPL
